dependencies: Large quantities of commodities including dairy products, meat products, tomato products, sugar and other sweeteners, soybean and vegetable oils, coffee beans, wheat and processed grains, eggs, and other fruits and vegetables
Significant quantities of plastics, cardboard, resin, glass, and metal for packaging
Electricity, diesel fuel, and natural gas for manufacturing and distribution
Reliance on third-party brokers, agents, and distributors for sales
risks: Volatility in commodity, energy, and other input costs
Severe weather or global climate change
Consumer, industrial, or investment demand changes
Changes in governmental regulation and trade, tariffs, alternative energy, and agricultural programs
Inflationary pressures in procurement, manufacturing, and logistics costs
Inability to maintain favorable arrangements with suppliers
Termination of relationships with significant contractual counterparties including licensors
Labor shortages and increased labor costs
Disruptions from natural disasters, labor strikes, cybersecurity incidents, or pandemics affecting suppliers or distributors

regulatory_risks: Compliance with FDA, USDA, FTC, Department of Labor, Department of Commerce, and EPA regulations in the United States
Compliance with food safety, health and safety, anti-corruption, and data privacy laws in international markets
U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) compliance
U.K. Bribery Act compliance
U.S. Trade Sanctions Reform and Export Enhancement Act compliance
Various anti-corruption regulations in regions where the company operates
Environmental regulations including Clean Air Act, Clean Water Act, Resource Conservation and Recovery Act, and CERCLA
CERCLA joint and several liability for closed, inactive, or divested operations
Changes in tax laws and interpretations including OECD two-pillar plan and global minimum tax of 15%
Pillar Two legislative interpretation uncertainty
State tax law changes
Transfer pricing regulations and IRS examinations
Income tax audits and uncertain tax positions
Antitrust and competition laws in the United States, Canada, European Union, United Kingdom, and elsewhere
Data privacy laws including GDPR, CCPA, and CPRA
Product labeling and marketing regulations
Advertising and consumer promotion restrictions
Climate change regulations including carbon pricing or carbon tax
Sustainability disclosure requirements from SEC, European Union (Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive and Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive), and California
Forced labor regulations including Uyghur Forced Labor Prevention Act (UFLPA)
Packaging regulations including PFAS restrictions
Trade and export controls
Foreign ownership restrictions and nationalization risks
Local statutory requirements governing pension and retirement plans
Employee benefit laws and regulations
Occupational safety and health regulations (OSHA)
Environmental remediation requirements
Product liability and food safety regulations
Intellectual property protection laws
Securities laws and SEC reporting requirements

sanction_risks: U.S. sanctions on Russia affecting business operations and asset transfers
Restrictions on transfer of funds to and from Russian entities
Potential loss of Russian assets or inability to conduct business operations in Russia
Economic sanctions and export controls on certain industry sectors and parties in Russia
UFLPA prohibiting import of goods from Xinjiang Uyghur Autonomous Region of China
Risk of materials and products being held by U.S. Customs and Border Protection based on suspicion of forced labor origins
Regulatory penalties for failure to comply with sanctions
Trade barriers, tariffs, and quotas affecting imports and exports
Discriminatory or conflicting fiscal policies in foreign jurisdictions
Capital controls and foreign currency exchange controls
Economic sanctions and export controls affecting cross-border product distribution
